title: Marbella Week Luxury Yacht Rental 2026 Costs and Tips
html:
Marbella Week transforms the Costa del Sol into a playground for the superyacht set, with marina berths filling fast and day rates for a crewed 50-foot motor yacht starting around EUR 4,500. Whether you join the social flotilla or explore quieter coves, renting a boat through BoatHire24 keeps your plans flexible and secure.

What is Marbella Week and where do you set off?

Marbella Week is an open social event in late summer, not a regatta, where yacht owners and charterers gather in Puerto Banús and nearby anchorages. The week typically runs in the last full week of August and draws a glamorous crowd. The main departure points are Puerto Banús itself, the Real Club Náutico de Marbella in the town centre, and the smaller marina at Cabopino to the east. West along the coast you find the well-sheltered Bay of Estepona and the port of Sotogrande, both popular for longer cruises.

Many skippers head to the beach clubs at Nikki Beach or Ocean Club, anchoring a few hundred metres offshore. For a quieter day, motor east towards the Cala de Mijas or the rocky coves near Marbella’s La Concha mountain. Distances are short, Puerto Banús to Cabopino is about eight nautical miles, so you can explore several spots in a single day.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Marbella Week?

Spain generally requires a recreational boating licence for bareboat charters, but crewed yachts include a professional skipper and need no personal licence. If you plan to drive the boat yourself, you should hold a Spanish PATENTE or an International Certificate of Competence (ICC). The operator on BoatHire24 will confirm exact requirements at booking, as some smaller hire boats accept a simple online test. For luxury rentals, a skipper is almost always included.

During Marbella Week, anchor close to the shore to watch the yacht parades; the best view is from the eastern end of Puerto Banús breakwater.

Which boat to rent for Marbella Week?

  1. Azimut 58 flybridge motor yacht, ideal for onboard socialising with a large aft deck, sleeps up to ten guests, from EUR 5,500 per day.
  2. Fairline Squadron 50, a classic British sports cruiser perfect for day trips to Cala de Mijas, capacity eight guests, from EUR 4,200 per day.
  3. Lagoon 42 catamaran, stable and spacious, excellent for families or groups wanting a sailing experience, up to twelve guests, from EUR 3,800 per day.
  4. Axopar 37 open tender, a fast, stylish runabout for short harbour hops, maximum six guests, from EUR 1,900 half-day.
  5. Sunseeker Manhattan 66, a flybridge motor yacht with three cabins, perfect for a crewed week-long charter, up to ten guests, from EUR 7,200 per day.

If you are drawn to the Azimut line, you can browse the Azimut 58 charter page for availability and exact specifications for 2026.

How much does it cost to rent a yacht in Marbella Week?

Crewed luxury motor yachts start at EUR 4,000 per day in 2026, while a half-day on a small speedboat costs from EUR 600. The table below breaks down typical price bands.

  • Shared tour on a rigid inflatable: from EUR 120 per person for three hours.
  • Small motorboat half-day (six guests): EUR 650, EUR 900.
  • Catamaran full-day (twelve guests): EUR 3,200, EUR 4,500.
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day (eight to ten guests): EUR 5,000, EUR 8,000.

You can compare prices and book verified day charters in Marbella Week through the BoatHire24 platform before demand peaks.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 600, EUR 8,000 per day.
  • Peak months: August (especially the last week).
  • Trip length: typically 4, 8 hours.
  • Guest capacity: 6, 12 guests.
  • Currency: EUR.

Questions about boat rental in Marbella Week

Can I rent a yacht for just one day during Marbella Week?

Yes, most operators offer full-day charters, and many also allow half-day bookings for smaller boats.

Should I book a catamaran or a motor yacht for the week?

Motor yachts are quicker for reaching far anchorages, while catamarans offer more deck space and stability for large groups.

What happens if the weather is bad during Marbella Week?

The skipper decides whether it is safe to sail; in summer the Mediterranean is generally calm, but afternoon Levant winds can build from the east.

How far in advance should I book a luxury yacht for Marbella Week?

For the most popular models and dates, booking two to three months ahead is wise, as the week attracts a high volume of charterers.

Whether you are planning a glamorous day hopping between beach clubs or a quiet family cruise, booking through the BoatHire24 marketplace gives you access to verified listings, transparent pricing, and hosts who know the Marbella Week scene. If you own a yacht, you can list your boat and start earning from this high-demand period in 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella Week and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
